title: Generics 2
description: You can implement methods on generic structs. The impl block must also declare the type parameter.
instructions: Implement the methods for the generic Wrapper struct.
hints: The `impl<T>` declares T as a type parameter for the implementation block.
Self is an alias for the type being implemented (Wrapper<T> in this case).
This code compiles. Study how generic impl blocks work.
explanation: When implementing methods on a generic struct, you must declare the type parameter on the `impl` block: `impl<T> Wrapper<T>`. The `Self` type alias refers to `Wrapper<T>` within the impl block. You can also write specialized implementations for specific types: `impl Wrapper<i32>` would only apply when T is i32.
